config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary, because once the state or the majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes very hard to stop. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting personal dignity, freedom, and due process, even when that is inconvenient. Collective interests are important, but they should be limited by clear rights that cannot be casually sacrificed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya sangat peka terhadap risiko ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama”, karena sejarah di sini menunjukkan bahwa hak sipil bisa mudah terkikis kalau tidak dijaga keras. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan publik dan keadilan, tetapi hak individu harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” bisa berubah menjadi alasan yang nyaman untuk menekan kebebasan orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “public interest” can become a justification for silencing weaker people. Society does matter, but if individual rights are treated as optional, then the majority or the state can excuse abuse in the name of order, morality, or stability. For me, the better principle is that the common good should be pursued within firm limits of basic rights, due process, and human dignity. A society is not truly healthy if it protects itself by sacrificing the very people it is supposed to serve.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena bagi saya itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Dari pengalaman politik di Amerika, saya melihat bahwa ketika pemerintah atau mayoritas diberi ruang terlalu besar atas nama ketertiban atau keamanan, hak sipil justru yang paling cepat tergerus. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti darurat publik, tetapi bahkan di situ hak dasar harus dibatasi seketat mungkin dan diawasi keras. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa merampas martabat dan kebebasan pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how “public interest” can become a convenient excuse for silencing critics, protecting the powerful, or pressuring minorities, so I do not trust that phrase when it is left too broad. The common good matters, especially in crises, but it must still be constrained by due process, human dignity, and basic rights that the state and majority cannot casually override. A society that is willing to sacrifice individual rights too easily is not stronger; it is just more dangerous.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami cukup jelas menunjukkan bahwa ketika pemerintah atau mayoritas diberi alasan “demi kebaikan bersama,” kebebasan sipil sering menjadi korban paling awal. Memang ada keadaan darurat yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, sementara, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengikis hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat dan kebebasan pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
